payload.intent: Produce the first complete SVG illustration of the mechanical pocket watch, implementing all specified components: the metallic case with crown and chain, the cream dial with Roman numerals and tick marks, the three hands set to 10:10:35, the sub-dials for small seconds and power reserve, and the visible movement gears in the lower half, all centered in a 600x600 viewBox with realistic shading and valid XML structure.

payload.critique: The artifact fails to render the visible mechanical movement (gears and balance wheel) in the bottom half of the watch, leaving the area as a solid dial face instead of a cutaway view. Additionally, the hour and minute hands are incorrectly positioned at 12:00 instead of the specified 10:00 and 2:00 positions. To fix this, you must implement a cutaway section in the lower dial area to reveal the three gears and balance wheel, and rotate the hour hand to the 10 o'clock position and the minute hand to the 2 o'clock position.

payload.intent: Refine the spec to explicitly mandate a cutaway view for the movement and clarify the time setting to 10:10, ensuring the generator addresses the missing gears and incorrect hand positions.

args.command: cd /src/theloop/runs/20260430-211804-watch-spec/workspace && python3 -c "import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; tree = ET.parse('artifact.svg'); print('Valid XML'); root = tree.getroot(); print(f'Root tag: {root.tag}'); print(f'Children: {len(root)}')"

payload.critique: The artifact is missing the 60 minute tick marks around the outer edge of the dial, which is a specific visual requirement for the watch face. Additionally, the bezel ring is only partially distinct, and the power reserve arc's exact percentage is ambiguous. To improve the score, add the 60 minute tick marks to the dial face.

payload.intent: The primary defect is the complete absence of the 60 minute tick marks on the dial face, which is a specific visual requirement. The iteration will focus on adding these ticks and ensuring the thinner hour markers at non-cardinal positions are rendered, as they were previously missing or indistinct.

args.command: sed -i 's/y1="132" y2="137"/y1="130" y2="135"/g' artifact.svg && sed -i 's/stroke="#6a5a30" stroke-width="0.8"/stroke="#4a3a18" stroke-width="1"/g' artifact.svg
grep -c 'y1="130" y2="135"' artifact.svg && grep -c 'y1="132" y2="137"' artifact.svg
python3 -c "import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; ET.parse('artifact.svg'); print('Valid XML')"

echo "=== Hands (10:10) ===" && grep -n 'hourHand\|minuteHand\|secondHand' artifact.svg | head -5 && echo "=== Sub-dials ===" && grep -n 'powerReserve\|smallSeconds' artifact.svg | head -5 && echo "=== Cutaway ===" && grep -n 'movementClip\|dialClip' artifact.svg | head -5 && echo "=== Chain ===" && grep -n 'chain' artifact.svg | head -3 && echo "=== Roman Numerals ===" && grep -c '<text' artifact.svg

payload.critique: The cutaway window design obscures the bottom half of the dial, making the minute tick marks and hour markers at 6, 7, 8, and 9 o'clock invisible or ambiguous, which violates the requirement for 60 distinct ticks and 12 visible hour markers. To fix this, the cutaway should be redesigned as a smaller, offset window (e.g., at 4-5 o'clock) or the dial elements should be rendered on a layer above the cutaway background so all 60 ticks and 12 markers remain fully visible and distinct.

payload.intent: The cutaway window is moved from the bottom half to the lower-right side (4 to 5 o'clock position) to ensure all 12 hour markers and 60 minute ticks remain fully visible and distinct on the dial face, resolving the occlusion defect identified in the previous critique.
